page: Amazon Web Services m8i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. This server features 48 dedicated vCPUs, corresponding to 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a large 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512).

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across memory and compute tasks. The instance achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2209.0 and a multi-core score of 19971.0. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 3825.86 GB/sec and large block uncached bandwidth at 195.84 GB/sec. In database and application workloads, the server demonstrates high throughput, processing 89.2 million Redis SET operations per second and achieving a static web serving throughput of 79.6 GB/sec. Additionally, the built-in AMX instructions support efficient CPU-based machine learning, delivering 255.72 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 7B LLM.

The instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning workloads requiring high-speed local disk I/O or heavy GPU acceleration must rely on network-attached storage and CPU-based execution. However, the high resource density of DDR5 memory and dedicated Xeon cores offers a cost-effective profile for general-purpose enterprise applications. This server is well-suited for workloads such as database operations, web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of DDR5 RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, it leverages the Nitro hypervisor and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance delivers strong performance across diverse benchmarks, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2209.0 and a static web serving throughput of 79.6 GB/sec. While it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, its high memory bandwidth and dedicated compute resources make it a highly capable platform for database operations, web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix acceleration for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
Equipped with 192.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix math
Delivers 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware
Suited for database operations, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
